summary: The Cloudflare stack workflow now fails closed before Pages deployment unless the deployed replay-reservation Worker returns exactly one 201 winner and seven 409 replay responses with one consistent reservation identity.

verified_behaviors: accepts exactly one winner with consistent replay receipts
executes bounded concurrent requests through an injected transport

design_implication: Pages deployment is now ordered after Worker health and one live atomicity proof, reducing the chance of publishing a research surface backed by a non-atomic or misrouted replay reservation service.
uncertainty: The live probe has not run because Cloudflare credentials and a deployment identity are not available in this run.
One successful probe covers one generated reservation key and does not establish universal availability or indefinite storage durability.
Wrangler deployment-url output remains unobserved in a real run.
falsification_routes: Dispatch the stack workflow with configured Cloudflare credentials and inspect cloudflare-replay-reservation-concurrency-proof.json.
Cause two simultaneous requests to receive 201 or return divergent reservation IDs; the verifier must reject and prevent Pages deployment.
Redeploy the Worker and repeat the same reservation key to test persistence across deployment separately.
privacy_boundary: Contains repository paths, commit identities, infrastructure contract details, and synthetic probe behavior only. No private chat, health, credentials, contact data, or diagnosis/treatment claims are included.
